item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God I hate the way I wrote this. Might have to give it a rework some time because, like the rest of my work from 2014, it is janker than jank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so the 6 meters in diameter is for the measurement of its body. Just imagine a really big snake. The 2 meters is for a growth at the end of the snake where all the heads pop out of.

So you have big snake, skinny neck, tons of heads.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite several references to it in the comments, I am still totally confused as to how a snake is 6 meters in diameter. Can we get more of an explanation? Is it just a big circle of snake with heads coming off of it? How is part of it 2m across? The structure of the creature is unclear. Or maybe it's just me, and in that case, carry on.

Is Hachi disease supposed to be Hashimoto's thyroiditis?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this article reminded me of another article about multi-headed creatures in Japan, which is SCP-2478. This article exists within the right geographic area, and has a related theme.

Proposing editing this:

All bodily fluids, including blood, spinal fluid, saliva and mucus have been shown to contain some trace of Hachi disease in human subjects, and in the case of SCP-1645, its venom appears to have been replaced by a serum containing Hachi disease.

To this:

All bodily fluids, including blood, spinal fluid, saliva and mucus have been shown to contain some trace of Hachi disease in human subjects, and in the case of SCP-1645, its venom appears to have been replaced by a serum containing Hachi disease.1

If anyone has any other idea, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Hachi disease has been discovered in the bodily fluids of SCP-2478, suggesting that it may have been utilized in the creation of that anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It'd probably work better for me as a humanoid scip. No vote.

Zaphod Beeblebrox, anyone?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, you are right… I haven't been here for while…
I'll ask him next time, but not today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was something on the site that I read a while back that claimed that Prometheus Labs found the original sample of Telekill Alloy in old Greek archeological sites. Metals. Alloys. People of Greek descent are sometimes immune to the venom. I'm not sure if you meant it or not, but I am officially headcanoning that this thing generates Telekill Alloy.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: I've since reverted these edits, as they were made without permission from the current author. While you did come up with the idea for the article and all that, the current version was written by Ihpkmn, and as such you need to obtain permission from him before you make substantive edits to the work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made a small edit regarding the wheel like shape that ihpkmn implied, just to prevent any more confusion.
To further clarify it, the reason why SCP-1645 is 5 meters wide is because it is 5 meters wide in diameter. SCP-1645 is radially symmetrical, and its heads are distributed to all of the hub's sides. In other words, it is kinda like a reptilian sea star.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pretty cool. It reminds me Yamatano-orochi of Japanese myth and Hydra of Greek myth. I love the modern mythological stuff.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just one question. How big is SCP-169? Is is some massive colossus of a snake, or is it that big because it's body is shaped like a wheel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what's behind the expunged, original head removal scenario? Many things actually, but something like that is not mentioned anywhere else in the article so it leaves me hanging. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that's not made clear at all, I also got the impression it was referring to a cross-section of the body. Needs work in that regard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll also note that the cancer which causes the Polycephalism is also separate from the virus. The virus itself just seems to mostly kill people via immunodeficiency, but is affecting the snake differently for whatever reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So….. does Procedure-1645-August involve specially brewed sake?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta say, I agree that the disease is really the skip here. I could see the choice in making the core designation the snake, but Hachi disease really deserves to be 1645-1 at the very least.

Also, there's this…

Amata-Gan's results in tumor-like growths gradually forming next to the heads of organisms affected by Hachi disease, which eventually sprout into fully functional, additional heads complete with vascular, nervous and digestive systems. The growth of structures caused by Amata-Gan's process can be accelerated if the subject afflicted by Amata-Gans engages in any physical activity, including movement. These growths eventually branching off into their own distinct heads, complete with neural tissue and bone structures; these heads are sentient, and in the case of human beings, sapient.

Is this repetition intentional?

I'm not really thrilled with it. the tie to Japanese mythology rather than some western mythos raises my eyebrows, though, and nearly saves the article… Nearly.

0

E: That reminds me… The [DATA EXPUNGED]. I really wish you'd toss us a bone here. It could be really interesting to know what's going on there, but unfortunately we can only guess…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome article I like the migrational desiease infested snake hydra

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part of me wants to nitpick some of the terminology, but this is just way cool. I totally got the references, or at least I think I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: psssst… Kusanagi no Tsurugi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say because the snake seems to be the originator of the disease and its ability to produce what appear to be swords from its body.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see a good reason why the skip is the snake and not the virus, other than the fact that the original was about the snake. In the current version, the snake seems abnormal, but not anomalous.

I'm not a fan of expungement here.

All the references to Greece and a needle from weird alloys, I don't get what they're referring to, and the rest didn't make me care enough to go and search for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I don't know why but this sort of body horror is really squicky to me… it's making me itchy and ill. Especially the idea of a snake shaped like a wheel. Urrrrgh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it enough to up vote, and especially liked the bit about the diet. However, the Greek alloy thing seemed more like an unnecessary "wink wink Hydra connection, geddit!?"than anytning else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for rewriting SCP-1645. Though I wish that it would still keep the nickname "Yamata No Orochi", but at least you kept its regenerative and head-sprouting abilities. I'm thankful for what you did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ihpkmn said in chat that the snake was shaped like a wheel with the heads as the spokes attached in a radially symmetrical pattern to the hub, IIRC.

Overall, this is technically well put together, but I agree that it isn't doing much for me at the moment. Neutralvote.

EDIT 2020: You have shit taste, 17yo me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
6-meter diameter

This is a circle six meters wide. How big is your snake? Do you mean length? A six-meter-long snake is terrifying enough - a six-meter-wide Mamushi would be as tall as a house and nearly two hundred meters long.

heads do not branch off from the spine, but are instead connected to a 2m diameter "hub" structure in the center

The hub is smaller than the rest of the snake?

Despite the confusion over mathematical terms here, I much prefer this to the original. Nice Hydra/Orochi crossover stuff, I like the "all myths are true" vibe. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the case that the original head of the subject has been removed, [DATA EXPUNGED].

I think this is rather unnecessary expungement. If you're just gonna expunge it just don't do it.

Overall, much better, but I don't dig the core idea. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so orochi had a baby with the hydra?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's quality writing and I dig the implication of the consequences of this thing's condition spreading to human beings, but ultimately it doesn't do much for me. It'd probably work better for me as a humanoid scip. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 6 meter diameter snake in a 5 meter high terrarium? I think something's wrong there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a specially modified terrarium

I'm curious, what kind of modifications? Given the size of this thing, something as simple as leaning on the glass would break it.

as well as administer antibiotics

Here's where you lost me. You say that antibiotics are to be issues, but later on, you say that Hachi disease is viral, and further on you say those bitten contracted fungal growths. I'm not sure what Hachi's even is. Viral? Fungal? Bacterial? A combination of the three?

Biohazard protocols

A little more can be explained in this regard. Does something as simple as a decontamination shower work, or does it require full hazmat gear?
I might just be being picky on this particular point, but biohazards, especially Foundation-tier biohazards, don't seem like something you want to skip over in containment.

with a 6-meter diameter

Holy balls that's a big snake. If that's just how wide it is, then the length from nose to tail makes it ridiculously gargantuan. This is the point I was talking about in my first issue up top. I think you were speaking of size in terms "in length" instead of "in diameter". Your biggest anacondas have a diameter to length of about 20:1. That means this snake's length would be about 120 meters, far larger than the tank in question will reliably allow.

Overall… eh. I'm not even on the middle ground myself. I like snake SCPs well enough, but there's not really enough to draw the eye for me and the inconsistencies really detract the overall enjoyment. I am admittedly curious about the metal aspects of the snake, but those are pushed into the end as almost an afterthought. I'm sorry, but thumbs down from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems to have a lot of unnecessary information and doesn't really grab my interest. No vote right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All of those errors have been addressed. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! I've always been fond of snakes, and I think this offers an interesting different perspective.
also, mamushiiiiiiiii

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can be accelerated These growths eventually branching

Punctuation / capitalisation issue

completely with neural tissue and bone structures

Complete?

and all survivors were SCP-1645 was recovered

Missing something here

Dr. McGinn discovered a scar the "hub" portion

And here

I know I could have fixed some of these myself but I've gotten shit before for making corrections. It's a +1 from me even though I'm not too sure if I understand what bigger picture is being hinted at in the final addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, damn. This alarmingly close to the hydra SCP I'm working on. Not identical, in that it lacks the distinctive gimmick that that one will have, but similar enough that I'm honestly not sure I can post mine. I can't downvote this in good conscience, but I'm hoping it doesn't make it. Sorry, Ihp, but I'm going to have to recuse myself from judgement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Went through it in chat, liked it then, like it now.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see a ton of differences between the two, albeit you executed it much better. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1645 was originally written by Crookera.

Gimme a second while I upload the original SCP and discussion pages in screencap form.

Original SCP
– hide block
Discussion page 1 and 2
– hide block












I apologize for any errors made in these pictures; author commentary coming when I have time tomorrow.
